results.title: [Literature Review] VideoMDM: Towards 3D Human Motion Generation From 2D Supervision
results.content:
Problem Formulation:
The objective is to train a 3D generative diffusion model $D\_\theta(x\_t, t, c)$ that samples realistic 3D motion trajectories $x \in \mathbb{R}^{J \times 3 \times F}$ (where $J$ is the number of joints, $F$ is the number of frames), optionally conditioned on a text embedding $c$, using only 2D supervision $y \in \mathbb{R}^{J \times 2 \times F}$ extracted from videos. This is challenging due to inherent depth ambiguity in monocular 2D data. The framework leverages a pretrained 2D-to-3D lifter $\mathcal{L}\_\phi$ to produce approximate 3D joint trajectories $\tilde{x}0 = \mathcal{L}\phi(y)$, which serve as a noisy teacher. The camera projection operator is denoted by $\Pi\_c(\cdot) = KR|t\_c$, mapping 3D joint coordinates to 2D. [...] Moonlight Logo
Moonlight Logo
Moonlight Logo

# [Literature Review] VideoMDM: Towards 3D Human Motion Generation From 2D Supervision

VideoMDM introduces a diffusion-based framework for training 3D human motion priors directly from accurate 2D poses extracted from monocular videos, eliminating the need for 3D ground truth. The core idea is to learn a coherent 3D motion manifold by combining approximate 3D estimates from a noisy teacher with robust 2D supervision. [...] Experimental Results:
VideoMDM is evaluated across three settings:

Ablation Studies:
Ablations confirm that multistep denoising and the representation alignment loss ($L\_{repr}$) are the most impactful components. Replacing the ray projection for $L\_{repr}$ with a naive 3D comparison triples the FID, highlighting the necessity of 2D-consistent pseudo-targets. Depth weighting for $L\_{pos}$ and the 2D velocity loss ($L\_{vel}$) also contribute measurable improvements.

results.title: I'm Tired of BROKEN Video Game Releases
results.content: just wasn't ready for prime time. And Minds Eye is just one example. We've seen other AAA releases recently come out in rough shape, then scramble with patches while their reputation just tanks. The pattern is exhausting. As consumers were tired of spending $70 or actually now $80 plus on a new game to only act as beta testers for the first few months with Monster Hunter Wild, it's especially disappointing because Capcom knows how to make good games. They've had several great game of the year contenders over the year and that, you know, attributes to their solid track record. That's partly why this situation is just so frustrating. Whether it was rushing to meet a fiscal quarter deadline or just underestimating the work that was needed for the PC launch, the result that squandered an [...] and fix later approach that big developers and publishers are taking with their games. Take Mind's Eye for example. This was another massively hyped up title in 2025. A sci-fi shooter from ex Rockstar game producers, and this was the GTA killer that they claimed. The game launched as a complete and total mess. Rampant with bugs, awful performance, steep stutters, and just a bland design overall. It quickly earned the shameful title of the worst game reviewed of 2025 so far with only about 38 positive user reviews on Steam. The leading complaints about Minds Eye. Yeah, you guessed it. Terrible performance issues and a drab unfinished feel. Sounds familiar, right? It's the same story of a big budgeted game faceplanting because it just wasn't ready for prime time. And Minds Eye is just one [...] Imagine one of the biggest launches of the year turning into one of the biggest disappointments we've seen in gaming. That's exactly what we're seeing with Monster Hunter Wilds. Capcom's latest entry into the beloved series stormed onto the scene with record blurb breakaking player accounts and sky-high expectations. But in a matter of a few months, the narrative has completely changed around it. Today, it's making headlines for all of the wrong reasons. from abysmal PC performance to baffling design and monetization choices just plainly disrespecting your customers. And it is beyond frustrating to see how common this is becoming and how developers seem like they just don't care. Let's discuss that in this video. Hey, if you enjoy content like this, drop a like, make sure to subscribe,

results.title: Video gamers' aggression linked to frustration, not violent content
results.content:
## Society & Culture

A group of high school students sits in a classroom at a table with laptops and papers while teachers help them with their work.
Soccer ball on a pitch with the stadium blurred in the background. The ball looks like the globe to indicate world history as it relates to the World Cup.
Patriotic-themed ‘America’s Greatest Hits’ vinyl album cover featuring collage-style images of famous American musicians, instruments, stars, an eagle, a jukebox, and a classic car in red, white, and blue tones beside a black record.
University of Rochester [...] Across the experiments, researchers found it was not the narrative or imagery, but the lack of mastery of the game’s controls and the degree of difficulty players had completing the game that led to frustration. The study demonstrated that aggression is a negative side effect of the frustration felt while playing the video game. “When the experience involves threats to our ego, it can cause us to be hostile and mean to others,” Ryan explains.

The researchers also surveyed 300 avid gamers to identify how real world gamers might experience the same phenomena. When asked about pre- and post-game feelings, gamers reported that their inability to master a game or its controls caused feelings of frustration and affected their sense of enjoyment in the experience. [...] Andrew Przybylski
Richard Ryan

The study is the first to look at the player’s psychological experience with video games instead of focusing solely on its content. Researchers found that failure to master a game and its controls led to frustration and aggression, regardless of whether the game was violent or not. The findings of the study were published online in the March edition of the Journal of Personalityand Social Psychology.

“Any player who has thrown down a remote control after losing an electronic game can relate to the intense feelings or anger failure can cause,” explains lead author Andrew Przybylski, a researcher at the Oxford Internet Institute at Oxford University, who said such frustration is commonly known among gamers as “rage-quitting.”

results.title: Video reviews are a game changer. Here's why. - HelpfulCrowd
results.content:
Customers don’t just want to know that you can solve their problem, they want to be sure that you can solve their problem better than anyone else. A video review is a great way to actually show this.

### Videos are desirable

Four times as many customers would rather watch a video about a product than read about it. That’s great news for any brand committed to harvesting video reviews from their customers. Video reviews are a great way to explain more about your products and their features too, and are ideal as an alternative or an accompaniment to salesy commercials and technical feature explainers.

### Videos are shareable [...] ### Video reviews create brand advocates

Customers that have had a positive experience with your company act as brand advocates when they share their positive feedback. This is the case for all kinds of reviews, but especially so for video reviews. Video reviews allow your reputation to be amplified in the best possible way, by someone else!

Customers and word of mouth marketing can really be your greatest brand champions.

### Get started with video reviews

HelpfulCrowd has been designed and optimized to help you gather customer video reviews and display them in the most effective places on your website. [...] If you still need convincing, here are eight reasons to encourage your customers to start sharing video reviews.

### Video reviews trigger an emotional reaction

While text reviews and star ratings are useful, there’s nothing quite like a video review when it comes to evoking an emotional trigger or reaction. Copy can often be rather sterile and many visitors to your store may not even bother to read endless reams of text.

When a customer is thankful, happy or relieved because of your product, potential customers will feel it too. This, in turn, compels the viewer to learn more about your brand and raises the chances that they’ll become a customer in future.
